article_title: Sungrazer Comet C/2026 A1 MAPS Faces Death or Glory as It Plunges Through the Solar Corona on April 4

content_preview.title: Sungrazer Comet C/2026 A1 MAPS Faces Death or Glory as It Plunges Through the Solar Corona on April 4
content_preview.summary: The farthest Kreutz sungrazer ever discovered will pass just 161,000 kilometers above the Sun's surface on Saturday, threading the inner solar corona in a make-or-break encounter that could produce a comet visible in broad daylight or end in total disintegration.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
Comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS), the farthest Kreutz sungrazer ever detected from the Sun at the time of its discovery, will reach perihelion at approximately 14:22 UTC on Saturday, April 4, passing just 0.0057 AU from the center of the Sun. That places its trajectory roughly 161,000 kilometers above the solar surface, deep inside the Sun's corona, where temperatures exceed one million degrees Celsius.

The comet was [discovered on January 13, 2026](https://www.universetoday.com/articles/new-sungrazer-co...

editor_notes.content_quality: Engaging science article with rich historical context and clear explanation of survival prospects.
editor_notes.source_verification: Space.com: verified — perihelion April 4, 0.0057 AU, 161,000 km, SOHO LASCO C3, Lovejoy precedent confirmed. Universe Today: discovery details verified (Jan 13 2026, 2.056 AU, Atacama, co-discoverers, mag 17.8). Potential discrepancy: article states JWST nucleus ~400m but UT extraction suggests 2.4 km. Also 371 BC vs 362 BC for progenitor. Live Science: behind paywall, general story confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts verified. Minor discrepancies in nucleus size and historical date may stem from WebFetch truncation.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Engaging but neutral. Evocative writing stays within acceptable limits.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh, timely coverage of imminent astronomical event.
concerns: Nucleus size discrepancy (400m vs 2.4km) — may reflect different measurements or WebFetch artifacts
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-written astronomy news. Minor factual uncertainties do not warrant blocking. Approved.
